questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Match the following List-I with List-II
questions.solution:
(P) $\mathrm{P}\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}_4}{\mathrm{~B}}\right)=\frac{\frac{1}{4} \times 1}{\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{10}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{3}{10}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{6}{10}+\frac{1}{4} \times 1}=\frac{1}{2}$
(Q) Required probability $=\frac{3 \times{ }^5 \mathrm{C}_3+5^3}{{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_3}=\frac{31}{91}$
$(R)$ Required probability $=\frac{3!(2)}{4 \times 4 \times 4}=\frac{3}{16}$
(S) $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A})=\sum_{\mathrm{i}=1}^3 \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{i}}\right) \cdot \mathrm{P}\left(\frac{\mathrm{A}}{\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{i}}}\right)=\frac{1}{3} \times 1+\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{6}=\frac{5}{9}$

Thus, $P\left(\frac{E_1}{A}\right)=\frac{\frac{1}{3} \times 1}{\frac{5}{9}}=\frac{3}{5}, P\left(\frac{E_2}{A}\right)=\frac{\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{2}}{\frac{5}{9}}=\frac{3}{10}$ and $P\left(\frac{E_3}{A}\right)=\frac{\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{1}{6}}{\frac{5}{9}}=\frac{1}{10}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $X=\{1,2,3, \ldots \ldots ., 10\} . A, B, C$ are three sets such that $A \subseteq X, B \subseteq X$ and $C \subseteq X$. Column-1: Contains types of three subsets of $X$. Column-2: Contains number of ways of selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 Column-3: Contains conditional probabilities $P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)$ or $P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)$ where $E$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 $E_1$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cap B)=5$ $E_2$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cup B)=5$. \(\begin{array}{lll} \text{Column-1} & \text{Column-2} & \text{Column-3} \\ \text{(I) } A \cap B \cap C \supseteq\{2,3,4,5,6\} \text{ and } A=B=C & \text{(i) } 32 & \text{(P) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=0 \\ \text{(II) } A \cup B \cup C=\{3,4,5\} & \text{(ii) } 242 & \text{(Q) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ \text{(III) } A \cap B \cap C=\{3,4,5,6,7\} \text{ and } A=B \neq C & \text{(iii) } 243 & \text{(R) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ \text{(IV) } A \cup B \cup C=\{6,7,8,9,10\} \text{ and } A=B \neq C & \text{(iv) } 343 & \text{(S) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=0 \end{array}\) [Note: $S \supseteq T$ denotes $S$ is a superset of $T$, means $S$ contains atleast all elements of $T$.] Question: Which of the following options is the only correct combination?
questions.solution:
(I) $\quad A \cap B \cap C=\{2,3,4,5,6\}$
Each element of 1, 7, 8, 9, 10 has only two choices
$\therefore \quad$ Number of ways $=2^5$.
(II) $A \cup B \cup C=\{3,4,5\}$
Each element of 3, 4, 5 has 7 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=7^3=343$
(III) $A \cap B \cap C=\{3,4,5,6,7\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 1,2, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
(IV) $A \cup B \cup C=\{6,7,8,9,10\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $X=\{1,2,3, \ldots \ldots ., 10\} . A, B, C$ are three sets such that $A \subseteq X, B \subseteq X$ and $C \subseteq X$. Column-1: $\quad$ Contains types of three subsets of $X$. Column-2: $\quad$ Contains number of ways of selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 Column-3: $\quad$ Contains conditional probabilities $P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)$ or $P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)$ where $E$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 $E_1$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cap B)=5$ $E_2$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cup B)=5$. \(\begin{array}{lll} \text{Column-1} & \text{Column-2} & \text{Column-3} \\ \text{(I) } A \cap B \cap C \supseteq\{2,3,4,5,6\} \text{ and } A=B=C & \text{(i) } 32 & \text{(P) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=0 \\ \text{(II) } A \cup B \cup C=\{3,4,5\} & \text{(ii) } 242 & \text{(Q) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ \text{(III) } A \cap B \cap C=\{3,4,5,6,7\} \text{ and } A=B \neq C & \text{(iii) } 243 & \text{(R) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ \text{(IV) } A \cup B \cup C=\{6,7,8,9,10\} \text{ and } A=B \neq C & \text{(iv) } 343 & \text{(S) } P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=0 \end{array}\) [Note: $S \supseteq T$ denotes $S$ is a superset of $T$, means $S$ contains atleast all elements of $T$.] Question: Which of the following options is the only correct combination?
questions.solution:
(I) $\quad A \cap B \cap C=\{2,3,4,5,6\}$
Each element of 1, 7, 8, 9, 10 has only two choices
$\therefore \quad$ Number of ways $=2^5$.
(II) $A \cup B \cup C=\{3,4,5\}$
Each element of 3, 4, 5 has 7 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=7^3=343$
(III) $A \cap B \cap C=\{3,4,5,6,7\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 1,2, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
(IV) $A \cup B \cup C=\{6,7,8,9,10\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Match the following
\(\begin{array}{l} \text{Column - I} & \text{Column - II} \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(A) Let } f(x) \text{ be a non negative continuous function } \\ \forall x \geq 0, f(0)=0 \text{ If } f(x) \leq f(x) \sin x \forall x \geq 0, \\ \text{ then the value of } f \left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}\right) \text{ is} \end{array} & \text{(p) 4} \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(B) If } \mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{x}^2+1\right)+12 \mathrm{x}+6-3 \mathrm{x}^2<\sin ^{-1} \\ \sin (100)+\cos ^{-1} \cos (100) \forall x \in R ; a \in I \text{ then a can not be equal to} \end{array} & \text{(q) 3} \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(C) If two events A and B are such that } \\ P\left(A^C\right)=0.35, P(B)=0.4 \text{ and } \\ P(A \cap B)=0.15, \text{ then } 20 P\left(\frac{B}{A \cup B^C}\right) \text{ is} \end{array} & \text{(r) 0} \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(D) The number of elements in the range of the function } \\ f(x)=\sqrt{\cos ^{-1}\left(4 x^3-3 x\right)-\frac{\pi}{2}}+\frac{(2 x+3)!}{\sqrt{x+2}} \text{ is /are} \end{array} & \text{(s) 5} \\ & \text{(t) 8} \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
(A) Let $g(x)=f(x) e^{\cos x}$
$\because g(x)$ is a decreasing function
$\therefore g(x) \leq g(0)=0$ i.e. $f(x) e^{\cos x} \leq 0$ but $f(x) \geq 0, e^{\cos x} \gt 0 \Rightarrow f(x)=0$
$\therefore f\left(\frac{3 \pi}{2}\right)=0$
(B)
$\begin{aligned}
& (a-3) x^2+12 x+6+a-\left(\sin ^{-1} \sin 100+\cos ^{-1} \cos 100\right) \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow a \lt 3 . \mathrm{D} \lt 0 \\
& 144-4(a+6)(a-3) \lt 0 \\
& \Rightarrow a \lt -9 \text { or } a \gt 6 .
\end{aligned}$
(C) $\mathrm{P}\left(\frac{\mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{A} \cup \mathrm{B}^C}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A} \cap \mathrm{B})}{\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \cup \mathrm{B}^C\right)}=\frac{1}{5}$.
(D) $\cos ^{-1}\left(4 x^3-3 x\right)-\frac{\pi}{2} \geq 0 \Rightarrow-1 \leq 4 x^3-3 x \leq 0$ i.e. $x \in\left[-4,-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right] \cup\left[0, \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right]$ also $\frac{(2 x+3)!}{\sqrt{x+2}}$ is defined $\forall x \gt -2$ and $(2 x+3) \in W$ $x=-1,0, \frac{1}{2}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A man has 3 coins $A, B$ and $C, A$ is unbiased, the probability that a head will result when $B$ is tossed is $\frac{2}{3}$, the probability that a head will result when C is tossed is $\frac{1}{3}$. One of the coin chosen at random and is tossed 3 times, giving a total of two heads and one tail.
\(\begin{array}{ll} \text{Column-I} & \text{Column-II} \\ \text{(A) The probability that the chosen coin was A} & \text{(p) } \frac{32}{75} \\ \text{(B) The probability that the chosen coin was B} & \text{(q) } \frac{1}{2} \\ \text{(C) The probability that the forth toss will result in head} & \text{(r) } \frac{209}{1296} \\ \text{(D) The probability that the forth toss will result in tail} & \text{(s) } \frac{9}{25} \\ & \text{(t) } \frac{215}{1296} \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
Let $E$ be the event of getting 2 head and I tail
$\begin{aligned}
& P(A)=P(B)=P(C)=\frac{1}{3} \\
& P(E / A)={ }^3 C_2\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2=\frac{3}{8} \cdot P(E / B)={ }^3 C_2\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^2\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)=\frac{4}{9} \\
& P(E / C)={ }^3 C_2\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)^2\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)=\frac{2}{9}
\end{aligned}$
So by Baye's theorem $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{E})=\frac{9}{25}, \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{B} / \mathrm{E})=\frac{32}{75}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Let $X=\{1,2,3, \ldots \ldots ., 10\} . A, B, C$ are three sets such that $A \subseteq X, B \subseteq X$ and $C \subseteq X$. Column-1: $\quad$ Contains types of three subsets of $X$. Column-2: $\quad$ Contains number of ways of selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 Column-3: $\quad$ Contains conditional probabilities $P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)$ or $P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)$ where $E$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ according to column-1 $E_1$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cap B)=5$ $E_2$ : Selecting three subsets of $X$ such that $n(A \cup B)=5$.
[Note: $S \supseteq T$ denotes $S$ is a superset of $T$, means $S$ contains atleast all elements of $T$.] Question: Which of the following options is the only incorrect combination?
questions.solution:
(I) $\quad A \cap B \cap C=\{2,3,4,5,6\}$
Each element of 1, 7, 8, 9, 10 has only two choices
$\therefore \quad$ Number of ways $=2^5$.
(II) $A \cup B \cup C=\{3,4,5\}$
Each element of 3, 4, 5 has 7 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=7^3=343$
(III) $A \cap B \cap C=\{3,4,5,6,7\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 1,2, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
(IV) $A \cup B \cup C=\{6,7,8,9,10\}$ and $A=B \neq C$
Each element of 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 has 3 choices
$\therefore$ Number of ways $=3^5$ but $A=B \neq C$
$\therefore 3^5-1=242$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (I) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{1}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (II) }]{\text { According to }} 0 \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (III) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P\left(\frac{E}{E_1}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5} \\ & P\left(\frac{E}{E_2}\right)=\xrightarrow[\text { (IV) }]{\text { According to }} \frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 3^5 \cdot 2^{10}}=\frac{31}{{ }^{10} C_5 \cdot 12^5}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
